data.title: Chevron fire at El Segundo refinery may spike gas prices, worsened by Newsom's foot-dragging on re-supply regulations.
data.description: In Los Angeles on October 3, 2025, a recent fire at the Chevron refinery in El Segundo is expected to cause a significant increase in gasoline prices. Governor Newsom's California Energy Commission (CEC) has failed to implement re-supply and minimum inventory measures leading to a predicted spike in prices. Details of the price surge following the refinery incident would be released once the impact analysis is complete.

data.title: Moss & Company leases up 490-unit luxury Sage LA at Koreatown-Hollywood with strong demand, opened in Aug 2024.
data.description: Moss & Company in Sherman Oaks, California, recently confirmed the full lease-up of Sage LA, a high-end apartment complex with 490 units located at 200 N. Vermont Avenue. Built by Hankey Investment Company, the property was ready for occupancy in August 2024 and has achieved stable occupancy since then, showcasing the high desire for contemporary, feature-filled living spaces in a prime area of Los Angeles. Situated on the outskirts of Koreatown close to Hollywood, Sage LA provides its tenants with access to various amenities.

data.title: Fitch Ratings confirms Akropolis Group's BB+ rating after acquiring Galio Group, maintaining stable outlook.
data.description: Last week, Fitch Ratings, a global credit rating agency, assessed Akropolis Group's acquisition of Galio Group, a real estate development and management company. The long-term borrowing rating of BB+ with a stable outlook that was initially issued to Akropolis Group in 2021 was re-confirmed following this transaction. This re-confirmation by Fitch Ratings highlights the stability and creditworthiness of Akropolis Group post-acquisition.

data.title: Mullen Group Ltd. to release Q3 earnings on Oct. 22, 2025, at 6:00 a.m. ET, with conference call and webcast.
data.description: Mullen Group Ltd. (TSX: MTL) based in Okotoks, Alberta, is set to announce its third-quarter earnings on October 22, 2025, at 6:00 a.m. Eastern Time. The Corporation has organized a conference call and webcast for the release.

data.title: Jeff Caruso joins Wrapbook to boost studio growth and advance production finance. AI tool helps teams save costs.
data.description: In Los Angeles on October 3, 2025, Wrapbook unveiled its latest innovation as an AI-driven force amplifier for production finance and accounting teams, enabling them to achieve more within limited budgets. The announcement included the appointment of Jeff Caruso as the former General Manager and Head of Sales for Film, Television, and Streaming at Cast & Company.

data.title: Tesla expected to reach $3 trillion valuation by 2026 per Wedbush analyst Dan Ives, up from $2 trillion.
data.description: A tech analyst, Dan Ives, is bullish on Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) predicting a rise in the company's valuation from $2 trillion to $3 trillion by 2026. The stock price of TSLA is currently showing movement. Ives mentioned that advancements in AI and autonomous vehicles will lead to a significant change in 2026, terming it as a historical moment. He recently raised his price target for Tesla shares from $500. For further information on this news, the full story can be found on a specific financial news platform.

data.title: Stock market futures up, gold at all-time high despite looming shutdown. Dow +0.27%, Nasdaq +0.44%, S&P 500 +0.34%. Gold $3,815.28.Risk of economic data blackout.
data.description: Stock market futures in the U.S. rose on Monday morning despite an 83% probability of a government shutdown, with Dow Jones futures up 127 points (0.27%), Nasdaq 100 futures gaining 108 points (0.44%), and S&P 500 futures rising by 0.34%. Gold also reached a new all-time high at $3,815.28 per ounce. The calm market reaction is attributed to the limited immediate financial impact of a potential shutdown compared to the risks of a prolonged political impasse. However, concerns remain about a blackout of crucial economic data that could leave investors and policymakers without essential information. Jim Bianco, President at Bianco Research LLC, stated that the market's initial response is rational given historical patterns of minimal shock.

data.title: US households facing increasing financial strain as grocery prices surge by 29%, dollar loses 25% value, affordability worsens.
data.description: American households are experiencing increased financial pressure due to rising costs of essential items, surpassing wage growth and diminishing purchasing ability. An Affordability Crisis is exacerbating as noted in The Kobeissi Letter, with basic necessities becoming more unaffordable. Since February 2020, core expenses have surged significantly, with grocery prices hitting an all-time high with a 29% increase, and overall food prices rising by 30%. Arbor Data Science data reveals that the cost burden extends beyond groceries, with food away from home prices surging by 33% and transportation services increasing by 36%. This leaves families with constrained budgets. The U.S. Dollar's purchasing power is also decreasing rapidly. Fed Chair's warning of "No Risk-Free Path" has led to an unusual outcome, with both Gold and Stocks hitting new records simultaneously. More details are available on Benzinga.com.
